intro: The biggest real split between Xero and Linear is lock-in posture: Xero carries medium lock-in (accounting data exportable), while Linear carries low lock-in (issue tracker, data exportable). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Xero is built for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and is not a fit for single-product apps with no ongoing user engagement. Linear is built for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and is not a fit for single-product apps with no ongoing user engagement. On the scoring side, Xero rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Linear rates 5/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Xero you accept that contact-based pricing scales steeply with list growth, while Linear comes with the reality that contact-based pricing scales steeply with list growth. Neither side is free. Xero's headline strength in the data is that centralizes customer data and automates lifecycle messaging.
quick_take: Xero is for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys; Linear is for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Xero if you are saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and can accept medium lock-in (accounting data exportable).
verdict_b: Choose Linear if you are saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and need low lock-in (issue tracker, data exportable).

faq.a: Xero uses a subscription model, while Linear uses a freemium model; Linear has a free tier and Xero does not. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Xero to Linear?
faq.a: Xero has medium lock-in (accounting data exportable); Linear has low lock-in (issue tracker, data exportable). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Xero; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both Xero and Linear score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is Linear a good alternative to Xero?
faq.a: Linear is built for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and explicitly not for single-product apps with no ongoing user engagement, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Xero for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
